title: WIP - CI Debugging
body:
For some reason we're having 8 images now instead of 9 as shown below.
Adjust the expected number in the e2e test.

```
"REPOSITORY                            TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE      R/O",
"docker.io/library/busybox             latest   db8ee88ad75f   12 days ago     1.44 MB   true",
"docker.io/library/redis               alpine   d975eaec5f68   2 weeks ago     52.3 MB   true",
"docker.io/library/alpine              latest   b7b28af77ffe   2 weeks ago     5.85 MB   true",
"docker.io/library/registry            2        f32a97de94e1   4 months ago    26.4 MB   true",
"docker.io/libpod/alpine_healthcheck   latest   c1103abb47d6   5 months ago    10.4 MB   true",
"quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx           latest   3ef70f7291f4   8 months ago    9.21 MB   true",
"quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels          latest   4fab981df737   9 months ago    4.68 MB   true",
"k8s.gcr.io/pause                      3.1      da86e6ba6ca1   19 months ago   747 kB    true",
```

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
It starts with commit f610a485c1bc. Need to check the caching.

Tracked it further down...
```bash
$ /home/valentin/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/bin/podman --root /tmp/crio --runroot /tmp/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /tmp --storage-driver vfs pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest

$ /home/valentin/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/bin/podman --root /tmp/crio --runroot /tmp/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/runc --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /tmp --storage-driver vfs save -o /tmp/fedora-minimal-latest.tar registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest

Error: unable to save "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest": Error copying image to the remote destination: Error writing blob: Digest did not match, expected sha256:b193eb6c1373eb5643cd96ec77eadc63d3748bc18386794d1c082a4b240946b0, got sha256:2570e965bc01b87098dceca6e4b94773e0143aa93b0bcc477437be4ff5cff255
```

There's a couple of issues:
1. The tests don't fail as errors from `pull` and `save` are currently ignored in `PodamanTestIntegration.CreateArtifact(...)`
~~2. It does **not** fail when leaving the run and runroot unchanged.~~
